basics.name: Joel Nicolow
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: I am currently an undergraduate at the University of Hawai‘i at Mānoa where I study Information and Computer Sciences. My specific interests include: data science, machine learning, and deep learning. I enjoy finding interdisciplinary applications for these interests.

interests.name: Data Science Applications in the Climate Sciences

work.company: Hawaiʻi Established Program to Stimulate Competitive research Change(HI)
work.summary: Undergraduate researcher using machine learning and data science tools to extract environmental information from images.

Introductory Remote Sensing Specialist at the Climate Resilience Collabrative in SOEST. Working with APIs and geospatial data in Python 3 including contour extraction and the use of machine learning toolkits for pixel classification.
highlights: Troubleshoot software, hardware and networking issues over the phone
Answer phone and email responses to the ITS Help Desk

work.summary: Conducting Geographic and environmental research that relies on deep learning image classification components to further understanding of the environmental conditions on Hawaiian mountains. Awarded Associated Students of the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa Research Award (Spring 2021). Using Python for data visualization, storage, and analysis including deep learning models that classify images based off of environmental conditions. Co-authored posters for the Hawaiʻi Conservation Conference and the American Geophysical Union.

work.company: CyberInfrastructure TRaining for Undergraduates in Summer (CITRUS) Hawaiʻi Data Science Institute
work.summary: One-month program developing key skills and abilities in CyberInfrastructure (CI) with a climate-focused research project applying CI skills. Skills developed in data science, analytics, and visualization.

work.company: University of Hawaiʻi - Thompson School of Social Work & Public Health
work.summary: Using the R programming language to aggregate observational data.
Undergraduate research assistant, member of the analytics team.

Conducted research under Dr. DeLay related to the environmental conditions on Hawaiian mountains. Used R and Python to visualize, store, and wrangle temperature, relative humidity, and other meteorological data. Co-authored published abstract for the American Geophysical Union.

Conducting research on taxation and diet in the Pacific. Awarded Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program Project Funding (Fall 2020). Creating an R framework to automate data acquisition and analysis. Presented UH Mānoa Undergraduate Showcase (Spring 2022)
Hawaiʻi Youth Conservation CORPS. Project presentation at Kupu Hoʻike.
Completed fellowship at the Behavioral Health Administration, Alcohol and Drug Abuse division. Compiled a complete list of substance use treatment providers in Hawai‘i.

volunteer.summary: I go to Hoʻokuaʻāina when I have avaible days because Hawaiʻi's food sustainibility and mālama ʻāina are very important to me.

awards.title: Research Award
awards.awarder: Associated Students of the University of Hawaiʻi
awards.summary: Research award obtained to finance trail cameras for meteorologic research on Mount Kaʻala

awards.title: Project funding
awards.awarder: Undergraduate Research Oppertunities Program, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa
awards.summary: Project funding for research on import taxation in the Pacific
